## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)

On June 4, 1959, Chakravarti Rajagopalachari, the massive, highly influential Indian independence activist, politician, and the absolute last Governor-General of India, aggressively founded the massive Swatantra Party. Deeply disillusioned by the massive, heavily socialist, and deeply centralized economic policies of Jawaharlal Nehru, the massive party fiercely advocated for absolute, free-market capitalism, the aggressive dismantling of the "License Raj," and massive protections for private property, becoming the most significant massive, right-wing opposition to the Congress in the 1960s.

On June 4, 2001 (Historical context note), the incredibly massive, devastating, and highly historic royal massacre in Nepal reached its absolute, tragic conclusion as King Dipendra officially passed away in a hospital. Following his horrific, massive shooting rampage that wiped out his family just days prior, he had been in a coma. His massive death aggressively elevated his uncle, Gyanendra, to the throne, deeply plunging the massive Himalayan kingdom into absolute political instability and massive, aggressive Maoist insurgency.
